name: Jack Frost
description: Jack Frost is a personification of frost, ice, snow, sleet, winter, and freezing cold. He is a variant of Old Man Winter, responsible for frosty patterns on windows, nipping noses and toes in winter, and the general onset of cold weather. He is often depicted as a mischievous sprite or an elfish figure, sometimes even a skeletal figure, wielding ice and cold as his tools. He is not inherently malevolent, but rather a neutral force of nature, sometimes helpful, sometimes harmful, depending on the circumstances. He is more a symbol of winter's power and beauty than a true villain. He appears in numerous stories and poems, particularly around the winter holidays, often as a bringer of winter's magic and a reminder of the season's beauty. His origins are somewhat obscure, likely evolving from older winter spirits and folklore, but he has become a well-established figure in modern English-speaking cultures, particularly in children's literature and popular culture.
subtitle: Personification of Winter's Cold Embrace
abilities: Illusions related to cold and ice
appearance: Jack Frost is often depicted as a small, elfish figure with pale skin and white or blue hair. His eyes are usually icy blue and sparkle with frost. He is often dressed in ragged, ice-covered clothing, sometimes with a hooded cloak. He may carry a staff or a branch covered in frost, which he uses to create his icy patterns and spread the cold. Some depictions portray him as a skeletal figure, representing the harshness of winter.
habitat: Jack Frost is most commonly found in cold climates, particularly during winter. He is associated with snowy landscapes, frozen lakes, and icy forests. He is often seen in areas where frost forms, such as windows, trees, and fields. He is not tied to a specific location, but rather travels with the cold weather.
behavior: Jack Frost is generally depicted as a mischievous but not malicious figure. He enjoys creating frosty patterns and nipping at people's noses and toes. He is a force of nature, indifferent to human suffering but also capable of bringing beauty and wonder. He is unpredictable and can be both playful and dangerous, reflecting the dual nature of winter. He is often seen as a solitary figure, wandering the winter landscapes alone.

keyMyths.title: Frosty Windows
keyMyths.description: Jack Frost is said to be responsible for the intricate frosty patterns that appear on windows during the winter. He uses his icy breath or fingers to create these beautiful designs, showcasing his artistic skill and the magic of winter.

sources.author: Katherine Briggs
sources.description: Provides an entry on Jack Frost and his role in folklore.
sources.text: Provides an entry on Jack Frost and his role in folklore.

sources.author: Charles Dickens
sources.description: Dickens mentions Jack Frost in his writings, contributing to his popular image.
sources.text: Dickens mentions Jack Frost in his writings, contributing to his popular image.
